title: Polythecium madagascariense
taxonomy: Polythecium madagascariense is classified within the family Ophioglossaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as adderstongue ferns. This species is native to Madagascar and is a type of fern. The classification of Polythecium madagascariense is further detailed at the genus and species levels, placing it within the genus Polythecium. At the kingdom level, it is classified as a Plantae, a group that encompasses all plants. Further classification includes it being a member of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns.
